action.reasoning: Observation: Iteration 13 confirmed ShortestPathFollower works — it reduced Euclidean distance to the human over 40 steps. All previous evals scored 0.0 nav_seek_success because (1) custom pathfinder branches were buggy, and (2) local controllers had oscillation/safety flaws. Analysis: The literature heuristic expert (ShortestPathFollower + local controller) achieves 1.00 success. I should use the built-in follower for long-range navigation instead of my custom pathfinder, and pair it with a clean proportional local controller that has proper zone clamps and a minimum forward boost in the sweet zone. The follower returns discrete actions; I map them to continuous base velocities (turn_left = negative ang_vel since positive = turn right/CW). Decision: Rewrite model.py with ShortestPathFollower hybrid + tuned local controller (target_dist=1.7, k_turn=5.0, k_fwd=4.0, sweet-zone minimum forward). Test locally with 2 episodes, then save checkpoint and submit eval if local results look promising (avg_dist < 3m and safe_steps in the hundreds).
